The test passes because the implementation already covers this case -- the condition `count === 3` on line 25 of `game-of-life.ts` includes any cell (live or dead) with exactly 3 neighbors. For a live cell with 3 neighbors, this means it survives, which is the correct Game of Life behavior.

Since the test passes without any changes to the implementation, there is no compilation error and no runtime error. This is expected because the survival-with-3-neighbors behavior was implicitly implemented as part of the reproduction rule (`count === 3` covers both reproduction of dead cells AND survival of live cells with exactly 3 neighbors).
